contraindications.clinicalRationale: Dapagliflozin requires adequate kidney function for efficacy and safety. FDA approval requires eGFR ≥25 ml/min per 1.73 m² based on clinical trial evidence and pharmacokinetic considerations.
contraindications.riskDescription: Reduced efficacy and potential for adverse effects with severe kidney dysfunction
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Other glucose-lowering agents appropriate for severe CKD
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Figure 7 — Dapagliflozin: 10 mg daily; FDA-approved eGFR ≥25 ml/min per 1.73 m². Trial enrollment: DAPA-CKD eGFR ≥25, DAPA-HF and DECLARE eGFR ≥30.

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Dapagliflozin approval for Type 1 diabetes has been withdrawn in Europe due to safety concerns, particularly increased risk of diabetic ketoacidosis. Only remains approved in Japan for T1D.
contraindications.riskDescription: Increased risk of diabetic ketoacidosis and other adverse events
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Standard insulin therapy and other approved T1D treatments
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Dapagliflozin had received approval for use as adjunct to insulin in T1D in Europe but the indication was withdrawn in 2021.

warnings.warningText: Monitor for diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), particularly in patients with diabetes
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor for signs and symptoms of DKA; discontinue if DKA occurs
Monitor for signs of genital infections; treat appropriately if they occur

warnings.warningText: Use with caution in patients with eGFR 25-30 ml/min per 1.73 m², as clinical trial experience is limited in this population
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients with eGFR 25-30 ml/min per 1.73 m²
warnings.actionRequired: Consider benefit-risk ratio; monitor kidney function closely
